current_backbone.description: Simplified conceptual 345-kV+ major ERCOT transmission corridors. This is a schematic approximation of major long-distance corridors, not exact geometry. Sources: ERCOT 2024 Report on Constraints and Needs (Dec 2024), ERCOT Planning Guide public maps.

corridors.name: West Texas Export Interface (Permian → Central TX)
corridors.notes: Major congested interface; #2 top constraint in ERCOT Oct 2023-Nov 2024 ($148M congestion rent). Carries wind/solar from Permian Basin to load centers.

corridors.name: Panhandle Interface (Panhandle Wind → DFW)
corridors.notes: Major wind corridor; Panhandle is primary wind generation hub. Ranked #6 constraint ($49M). Key DFW supply route.

corridors.name: North–South Spine (DFW to Houston)
corridors.notes: Interconnects major load centers; key reliability corridor.

corridors.name: DFW – Austin – San Antonio Corridor
corridors.notes: Central Texas spine serving major data center cluster in Austin/Waco/San Antonio region.

corridors.name: Gulf Coast Corridor (Houston → Corpus Christi)
corridors.notes: Industrial load corridor; Corpus Christi crypto loads.

corridors.name: Bell County – Salado Switch 138kV
corridors.notes: Top 7 constraint ($48M congestion rent Oct 2023-Nov 2024); Central Texas near data center corridor.

corridors.name: Tonkawa Switch – Morgan Creek SES 345kV
corridors.notes: Top 1 constraint ($156M congestion rent Oct 2023-Nov 2024); West Texas transmission bottleneck.

conceptual_765kv.description: CONCEPTUAL 765-kV backbone corridor — not exact geometry. ERCOT filed the Permian Basin Reliability Plan with PUCT July 2024; PUCT approved Oct 2024. Oncor filed Dec 2025 for Longshore Switch–Drill Hole Switch line (~180 miles). The 765-kV plan is under PUCT statewide decision process (decision target May 2025, now extended). This geometry is a schematic approximation based on public ERCOT filings. Sources: ERCOT Dec 2024 Report on Constraints, Utility Dive Jan 2026.

corridors.name: Permian Basin 765-kV Import Path (Conceptual)
corridors.notes: Conceptual corridor from Permian Basin (Midland-Odessa area) toward Central Texas load centers. Oncor's Longshore Switch–Drill Hole Switch segment ~180 miles is the first filed 765-kV project. Full corridor is conceptual pending PUCT approval. LABELED CONCEPTUAL — NOT EXACT GEOMETRY.

corridors.name: Permian Basin 765-kV Northern Branch (Conceptual)
corridors.notes: Conceptual northern branch of 765-kV plan toward DFW. Exact alignment subject to PUCT and ERCOT approval. LABELED CONCEPTUAL — NOT EXACT GEOMETRY.

major_substations.name: Odessa EHV Switch
major_substations.notes: Top 4 constraint area; Far West Texas
